title: Classical Ciphers and Cryptanalysis as Defect Labs · Runtime Contract: Crypto Go Bruteforce Cost Model
summary: A training tool presents Caesar, substitution, repeating-key XOR, and one-time pads as equivalent encryption choices and accidentally ships the toy implementation. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the bruteforce cost model increment in classical cipher attack and migration dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal deb…
objectives: Model offline and online search by keyspace, guess rate, parallelism, detection, lockout, value, and uncertainty without attacking unauthorized targets.
Explain one-time-pad key length, uniformity, secrecy, single use, distribution, and destruction requirements and their operational cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Bruteforce Cost Model · Theory Crypto Go Bruteforce Cost Model
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 6804 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to model an authorized classical-cipher attack while quarantining all legacy use to migration or defect labs; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model offline and online search by keyspace, guess rate, parallelism, detection, lockout, value, and uncertainty without attacking unauthorized targets.
